title: Channel Secret length docs differ from implementation

messages.author: mackenzie
messages.text: There's a discrepancy between the documentation and the actual implementation: Documentation https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/blob/main/docs/companion_protocol.md#4-set-channel Secret: 32 bytes (bytes 34-65) Total: 66 bytes Actual firmware (MyMesh.cpp): memcpy(&out_frame[i], channel.channel.secret, 16); i += 16; // NOTE: only 128-bit supported Secret: 16 bytes Total: 50 bytes Python library (reader.py): res["channel_secret"] = dbuf.read(16) # reads 16 bytes
messages.content_html:
There's a discrepancy between the documentation and the actual implementation: Documentation https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/blob/main/docs/companion_protocol.md#4-set-channel
  • Secret: 32 bytes (bytes 34-65)
  • Total: 66 bytes
Actual firmware (MyMesh.cpp): memcpy(&out_frame[i], channel.channel.secret, 16); i += 16; // NOTE: only 128-bit supported
  • Secret: 16 bytes
  • Total: 50 bytes Python library (reader.py):
res["channel_secret"] = dbuf.read(16) # reads 16 bytes
embeds_html:
MeshCore/docs/companion_protocol.md at main · meshcore-dev/MeshCore
A new lightweight, hybrid routing mesh protocol for packet radios - meshcore-dev/MeshCore

messages.author: mackenzie
messages.text: FYI @Liam Cottle didn't check which you went with for the mobile app?
messages.content_html: FYI @Liam Cottle didn't check which you went with for the mobile app?

messages.author: Liam Cottle
messages.text: channel secrets are 16 bytes, and those docs were ai generated by someone else and are definitely not accurate
messages.content_html: channel secrets are 16 bytes, and those docs were ai generated by someone else and are definitely not accurate
